article_title: Vienna Team Puts 8-Nanometer Sodium Clusters in a Schrödinger Cat State, Smashing the Quantum Macroscopicity Record by an Order of Magnitude
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exemplary physics submission with textbook Rule 9 discipline — PubMed link to the Nature paper itself cited directly alongside the U Vienna press release. Every specific verified verbatim: Pedalino as lead doctoral student, Arndt + Gerlich + Hornberger senior authors, Nature 649(8098):866-870 + DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-09917-9 + paper title 'Probing quantum mechanics with nanoparticle matter-wave interferometry' all in PubMed (s3); MUSCLE acronym + 8 nm diameter + >170,000 amu mass + μ = 15.5 macroscopicity + 'order of magnitude' improvement + GMBF10771 + FWF #32542-N grants in U Vienna (s1); 5,000 to 10,000 atoms cluster size in U Vienna (s1); Talbot–Lau configuration + 160 m/s velocity + 10-22 femtometre de Broglie wavelengths in Physics World (s2); 'large lump of metal / classical particle' Pedalino quote verbatim, 'one hundredth of a second / 100 million years' analogy in ScienceDaily (s0); Arndt 'open conundrum' quote verbatim in Physics World (s2); 10⁻²⁶ N force sensitivity in U Vienna (s1).

source-0.status: ScienceDaily. Verifies Pedalino quote verbatim and the 'one hundredth of a second / 100 million years' electron-equivalent analogy.

source-1.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): U Vienna release. Verifies MUSCLE acronym, 5,000-10,000 cluster atoms, 8 nm diameter, >170,000 amu, μ=15.5, order-of-magnitude framing, 10⁻²⁶ N sensitivity, GMBF10771 + FWF #32542-N grant numbers.

source-2.status: Physics World. Verifies Talbot–Lau configuration, 160 m/s velocity, 10-22 fm de Broglie wavelength range, Arndt 'open conundrum' quote verbatim.

source-3.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): PubMed listing of the Nature paper. Verifies Nature 649(8098):866-870, DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-09917-9, paper title verbatim, full author list (Pedalino + Ferstl + Hornberger + Arndt + Gerlich + Ramírez-Galindo).

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / What we know / Why it matters / What we don't know). Technical, precise, neutral. Length appropriate for News (~810 words). Strong Rule 9 discipline (Nature paper PubMed cited directly).
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the Vienna MUSCLE result. No archive collision.
recommendations: Add univie.ac.at and physicsworld.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — recurring physics-coverage outlets.

content_preview.title: Vienna Team Puts 8-Nanometer Sodium Clusters in a Schrödinger Cat State, Smashing the Quantum Macroscopicity Record by an Order of Magnitude
content_preview.summary: Sodium nanoparticles of 5,000 to 10,000 atoms diffract through ultraviolet laser gratings, reaching macroscopicity μ = 15.5 in a Nature paper from the Arndt group.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Physicists at the University of Vienna and the University of Duisburg-Essen have sent metallic nanoparticles containing thousands of sodium atoms through a matter-wave interferometer and recovered an interference pattern, demonstrating that solid lumps of metal heavier than 170,000 atomic mass units can still occupy a quantum superposition. The result, [published in *Nature*](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41566007/) under the title *Probing quantum mechanics with nanoparticle matt...
